{{Quote|Attention! This is Lando Calrissian. Attention! The Empire is taking control of the city. I advise everyone to leave before more Imperial troops arrive.|[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]|<ref name="ESB" />}}
The '''occupation of Bespin''' was a battle in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Bespin Wing Guard/Legends|Bespin Wing Guard]] under [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] (who turned to the side of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]) and the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] garrison under [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], which led to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] gaining control of the gas planet [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]].

==Prelude==
{{Quote|I had no choice. They arrived right before you did. I'm sorry.|Lando Calrissian to Solo and the other Rebels|<ref name="ESB" />}}
[[File:Security_Control_Center_TCG.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Bespin Wing Guards witness the Empire's arrival]]
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] won the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] and forced the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] offworld. The [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet's]] [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]],<ref name="kenner" /> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], pursued the Rebel leaders, in particular his son, [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. He hired the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and other [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] to find the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] carrying the Rebel leaders. Fett spotted the ''Falcon'' fleeing the [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] Imperial [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] and deduced its destination, [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] on the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Vader arrived before the ''Millennium Falcon'' and forged an initial agreement between himself and the [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. Calrissian would aid Vader in capturing the Rebels, in exchange for the continued freedom of his colony.<ref name="ESB" />

The ''Millennium Falcon'' entered and landed in Cloud City, piloted by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. They also had [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] on board. Its crew was greeted as welcomed guests by the Baron Administrator, who gave an especially warm welcome to his old friend, Han Solo (although not before initially coming across as hostile due to a past stunt that Solo pulled). They were given [[Yarith Bespin/Legends|accommodation]] in which to dwell while their ship was being repaired. After landing, C-3PO wandered off and was shot by a member of [[Gamma Squad (Imperial)|Gamma Squad]], dismantling him. C-3PO's remains were later sent to the recycling area of Cloud City, although they were intercepted by Chewbacca, who retrieved the parts. After a while, Calrissian invited them to a dinner arrangement, which turned out to be a trap, set by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader. Solo, upon discovering Vader in the dining room, attempted to shoot the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] with his blaster, only for Vader to effortlessly [[Tutaminis|absorb the bolts]] with his right hand, and telekinetically pull the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler's]] blaster away from him with his left. [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] surrounded the Rebels, ensuring their capture.<ref name="ESB" />

Later, [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Sheckil/Legends|Sheckil]] informed Lord Vader of C-3PO's destruction, and the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] visited the wreckage, remembering back when he had created the protocol droid as a young boy, and ordered the droid to be returned to Chewbacca while he prepared to interrogate Solo.<ref name="Thank">{{Tales|6|Thank the Maker}}</ref> He wanted to use Organa, Solo, and Chewbacca's capture as a lure to capture his primary objective, Skywalker. Indeed, Han Solo was [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] but not asked questions in order to trigger a response from Luke through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], sending him to rescue his friends.<ref name="ESB" /> The Imperials disabled the repaired ''Millennium Falcon's'' [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] to prevent its escape.<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

==The occupation==
===An altered deal===
{{Quote|I am altering the deal. Pray I don't alter it any further.|Darth Vader to Calrissian|<ref name="ESB" />}}
Vader had Solo frozen in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]], to test to see if the [[Carbon-freezing/Legends|freezing methods]] would effectively work on Skywalker. This was an important part of his plan; he would either take his frozen son to [[Palpatine/Legends|the Emperor]], or change plans and turn him into his own [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] for use against his [[Sith Master/Legends|master]]. He had Solo shipped to the gangster [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] by way of Boba Fett, who claimed Solo's [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]].<ref name="ESB" />

[[File:Not_yet_SWG_by_Steve_Argyle.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Luke Skywalker and Darth Vader duel.]]
This was a violation of his agreement with Calrissian, something Vader casually dismissed as an alteration of the deal. Shortly afterward, Vader's hopes were realized as Skywalker's ship was detected heading towards the [[City/Legends|city]]. As Vader left to prepare for his [[Duel on Cloud City/Legends|confrontation]] with the young Rebel, he gave clear instructions to Calrissian to keep Leia and Chewbacca on the planet, violating the agreement again.<ref name="ESB" />

Meanwhile, Skywalker had landed in the city and caught a glimpse of his friends being taken away by Imperial troops led by Lieutenant Sheckil. He was caught in a brief firefight with Boba Fett, who was protecting his cargo. Though [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] Leia managed to shout a warning to him, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Skywalker continued further into the city, ending up in a carbonite processing chamber where he [[Duel on Cloud City/Legends|faced]] Lord Vader in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]].<ref name="ESB" />

===Rebel escape===
[[File:Lando_turns_the_tables.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Lando Calrissian turns the tables on the Imperial troops.]]
Realizing that the situation was deteriorating, Lando Calrissian arranged for the [[Bespin Wing Guard/Legends|Bespin Wing Guard]] members [[Helder Spinoza/Legends|Helder Spinoza]], [[Corman Jeihn]], [[Pedar Solardo/Legends|Pedar Solardo]], [[Gir Endac]], and [[Razell Tameron]] to free Organa and Chewbacca from their Imperial escorts, resulting in Sheckil and his Imperial unit's arrest in the process. However, Organa and Chewbacca did not trust him due to his earlier betrayal of Solo, the latter also nearly killing him via strangulation, although Calrissian ultimately managed to relay that they still had a chance to save Solo from Boba Fett, causing them to decide to aid him and pursue the bounty hunter. While escaping, Calrissian alerted the populace to the Empire's takeover of the city and advised that they evacuate the city before more Imperials arrived. Calrissian's announcement likewise caused a mass-panic that aided the group in slipping past most Imperial patrols.<ref name="ESB" /> Organa ordered the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] to assist in opening doors for her to reach the ''Millennium Falcon.''<ref name="Rebel Strike" />

[[File:EscapefromCloudCity-Chewie3PO.png|thumb|right|300px|Chewbacca exchanges fire with stormtroopers]]
R2-D2 then tried to access sockets to Cloud City's main computer to access the doors, although thanks to C-3PO's poor advice, the astromech droid inadvertently electrocuted itself at one of the sealed doors by accessing a power outlet instead. While pursuing Boba Fett, the bounty hunter returned fire and taunted about how Solo was now his and no one would take his bounty.<ref name="Rebel Strike" /> Just after accessing the landing pad for the ''Millennium Falcon'', R2 also uncovered information from Cloud City's main computer that indicated that, although the hyperdrive was indeed fixed, it had also been deactivated by the Imperials, and attempted to warn them.<ref name="ESB" /> The ''Falcon'' then gave chase to ''Slave I'', disabling its shields in a vain effort to recover Solo.<ref name="Flight of the Falcon">''[[Star Wars: Flight of the Falcon]]''</ref>

The duel between Vader and Skywalker continued out onto an enormous wind tunnel, where Luke would [[Cho mai/Legends|lose]] his right [[Limb/Legends|hand]] to Vader's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and have his true [[Parent/Legends|parentage]] revealed. Even as Vader beckoned him to join his side and thus help end the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Luke stood defiant and chose to take his own life by falling from the platform they were on. As he plummeted towards the ground, he was sucked into one of the gas exhaust pipes and emerged below the city, grabbing onto a weather-vane.<ref name="UVG">''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]''</ref>

In a final attempt at survival, Skywalker [[Telepathy/Legends|contacted]] Princess Leia through the Force, who, having escaped in the ''Falcon'', now ordered the ship around to look for him. As they were rescuing Skywalker and taking off again, four members of the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]] squadron, [[Obsidian Squadron/Legends|Obsidian Squadron]], appeared off the tarmac and gave chase.<ref name="ESB" /> The ''Millennium Falcon'' fired back and shot down several TIEs.<ref name="Rebel Strike" /> The fighters had already been deployed for patrol duty even before the formal takeover of the planet.<ref name="CCG:CCL">{{CCG|set=Cloud City Limited|cardname=TIE Sentry Ships}}</ref>

Having escaped the planet, the ''Falcon'' now headed out into space, where it encountered Lord Vader's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] ,under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Piett]] in orbit. Attempting to flee into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], the crew found that Imperial technicians repaired their hyperdrive, but disabled it in the event of their escaping the city. As the ''Executor'' prepared to take the ship by [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]], R2-D2 managed to activate the hyperdrive, having been warned about the deactivation earlier by Cloud City's computer, and the ''Millennium Falcon'' escaped once again.<ref name="ESB" />

===Imperial takeover===
{{Quote|That planet is under the personal control of Darth Vader now.|[[Tyber Zann]]|<ref name="FoC" />}}
An Imperial strike force was formed to take over the city. The [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defenders]] and [[TIE/ca Punisher/Legends|TIE Interdictors]] cleared a landing zone in a break in the city's anti-air defenses and [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|fighter cover]]. The strike force, a mixed force of stormtroopers with repeaters, four [[Dewback trooper/Legends|dewback troopers]], five [[ASP-7 labor droid/Legends|ASP-7 labor droids]], deployed from the seven [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|landing craft]] and spread out. The landing craft also deployed four [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]], six [[All Terrain Personal Transport/Legends|All Terrain Personal Transports]], four [[Phase II Dark Trooper/Legends|Phase II dark troopers]], four [[Thermal detonator trooper (Galactic Empire)|Imperial Thermal Detonator Troopers]], and two Heavy [[Missile launcher trooper|Imperial Missile Launcher Troopers]]. The city had six [[Gravity Control Center]]s spread throughout, and the Rebels were planning on disabling them to scuttle the city and prevent its takeover. After landing, the strike force engaged [[T3-B heavy attack tank]]s sent their way and beat back [[Lando's Commandos]].<ref name="SWGB" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds}}
The Imperial strike force's bombers attacked a nearby [[tibanna gas platform]] and destroyed its storehouses to acquire additional resources. The strike force's [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droids]] established a base, and the force fought through the Rebel and the Bespin Security Force defenders, destroying storehouses in the process to acquire more resources. Walls prevented the strike force from accessing parts of the city; the strike force used their landing craft to reach those areas.<ref name="SWGB" />
{{Endgame}}

The strike force reached the Gravity Control Centers to prevent their destruction. The Imperials also destroyed the [[Bespin Government Center]] and built an [[Imperial Palace (Monument)|Imperial Palace]] in its place to cement their occupation.<ref name="SWGB" />

==Aftermath==
[[File:Leave_the_City.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Cloud City inhabitants flee the Imperial occupation]]
Skywalker, Organa, Chewbacca, Calrissian, and the droids returned to the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]], where Skywalker received a [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthetic]] hand.<ref name="ESB" /> With the Rebels defeated, the Empire took control of the city and the [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] gas mines. Cloud City and its remaining population had to endure the occupation of an Imperial garrison under the command of [[Hugo Treece]] for many [[Standard month/Legends|months]], until the [[Liberation of Bespin]],<ref name="TAB">''[[The Truce at Bakura]]''</ref> which would see the Empire driven out of the colony.<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> Nonetheless, there were some minor skirmishes prior to its liberation, such as a [[Raid on Bespin|raid]] by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] on the tibanna gas platforms shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="Leader">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]''</ref> and an internal [[Ugnaught rebellion|rebellion]] by the Ugnaught population caused by Treece unofficially turning Cloud City into a slave camp due to his embezzling funds.<ref name="Star Wars 56">[[Star Wars (1977) 56|''Star Wars'' (1977) 56]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The occupation of Bespin first appeared with the release of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].

The [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' presents the player with the opportunity to play through the occupation during the Darth Vader campaign. The campaign was also included in the game's [[2002]] expansion pack, [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']]. The player is tasked with establishing a base and securing the Gravity Control Centers. The player has the option of building additional units, and may also attack a mining platform for its resources. Half of the six Gravity Control Centers must survive and the player must destroy the Bespin Government Center to complete the mission.

In ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'', the Occupation of Bespin was a bonus mission called "Escape from Bespin." It branched off from one of the main missions in Wedge's campaign, [[Battle of Dubrillion (Galactic Civil War)|Guns of Dubrillion]], implying that the event was concurrent with the Battle of Dubrillion.

The battle also appeared in LEGO form in two missions in the [[2006]] video game adaptation of the [[Original trilogy]], ''[[LEGO Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy]]''. The missions are also included in its [[2007]] [[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga|compilation re-release]].
